acceptance_scenarios.scenario: A maintainer can open the English and Chinese alignment matrices and see the 0.80.2 document control, architecture analysis, local evidence anchors, three-layer dashboard, and opi-agent Partial status caused by the generic harness gap.

Current normative docs no longer describe .repo/pi-0.75.3 as the studied upstream baseline; .repo/pi-0.75.3 may appear only as historical prior-baseline context, while current-baseline statements name .repo/pi-0.80.2.
English and Chinese normative docs carry equivalent baseline, roadmap, alignment dashboard, future ecosystem, and non-goal statements for every Phase 9 documentation surface.

The roadmap consistently lists Phase 9 baseline realignment, Phase 10 core architecture deepening, Phase 11 tooling quality, Phase 12 provider correctness, Phase 13 session tree/context reconstruction, and Phase 14 TUI product polish; Models/Auth and AgentHarness are named as Phase 10 targets.

Future ecosystem candidates such as provider OAuth, broad provider catalog, image generation, custom extension UI, npm/gallery/update, web/share, provider hooks, and pi session migration are documented with entry conditions, not as scheduled near-term phase promises.
Docs guard tests reject current-scope claims for OAuth parity, image generation, custom extension UI parity, npm/gallery/update/enable/disable, web/share, and pi session compatibility.

The Phase 9 task diff changes only documentation and docs guard tests; it does not change production Rust source, Cargo manifests, runtime scripts, provider auth, image generation, custom UI, package ecosystem behavior, web/share behavior, pi session compatibility, or add an opi-types crate.

1.evaluator_summary: All 18 Phase 1 tasks passing. 213 tests green. Audit fixes committed (a66465a): SSE malformed event surfacing, CRLF parsing, BashTool cross-platform, interactive TUI wiring, auth error variant, config base_url/precedence, responsive cancellation. All cross-cutting gates pass: fmt, clippy, doc, smoke.
audit_notes: Audit note (2026-05-22): tasks 1.14, 1.15, 1.16 have evaluator_required=true in ledger but Opi-Evaluator: not-required in commit footers. Root cause: evaluator gate was run but determined these tasks did not require independent evaluation beyond mechanical gates (cli-runtime tier auto-flag is conservative). Commits are not amended; footer is authoritative for audit. Ledger evidence.opi_evaluator now mirrors the actual footer value.

2.evaluator_summary: All 16 Phase 2 tasks passing. 537 tests green. All cross-cutting gates pass: fmt, clippy, doc.

3.evaluator_summary: All 13 Phase 3 tasks passing. All cross-cutting gates pass: fmt, clippy, doc. Enterprise providers (Bedrock, Azure, Vertex) reviewed by code-reviewer with bugs fixed before commit.

audit_notes: Task 8.7 (final Phase 8 task): the independent D.2 evaluator initially returned PASS-WITH-CONCERNS with 3 confirmed medium guard-quality blockers in the new runtime_contract_docs.rs (tier not bound to surface; lib.rs cross-check bare-substring collision with AgentError/ToolDef; broad Chinese negation tokens 婵犵數濮烽弫鎼佸磻濞戞瑥绶為柛銉墮缁€鍫熺節闂堟稒锛旈柤?濠电姷鏁搁崑鐐哄垂閸洖绠伴柛婵勫劤閻捇鏌ｉ姀銏╃劸闂佽￥鍊曢埞鎴︽偐閸欏鎮欑紓浣插亾?濠电姷鏁搁崑鐐哄垂閸洖绠伴柛婵勫劤閻捇鎮楅棃娑欏暈妞ゎ偅娲熼弻宥夊传閸曨剙娅ｇ紓浣插亾?co-occurrence leaks). All 3 were fixed before commit (per-line surface->tier binding map; exact `pub use` re-export line pins; removed the broad tokens and rephrased ZH docs to narrow 濠电姷鏁搁崑鐐哄垂閸洖绠伴柛婵勫劤閻捇鎮楅棃娑欏暈妞?濠电姷鏁搁崑鐐哄垂閸洖绠伴柛婵勫劤閻捇鏌熺紒銏犳灍闁哄拋鍓熼弻鐔兼偋閸喓鍑￠梺?濠电姷鏁搁崑鐐哄垂閸洖绠伴柛婵勫劤閻挾鈧娲栧ú锕傦綖?tokens with the substring-co-occurrence limitation documented) and independently re-verified RESOLVED via mutation testing.

Residual (non-blocking, documented): the no_positive_claim substring helper shares a per-line co-occurrence limitation with the Phase 6/7 guards (a deceptive line placing a negation word next to a positive claim can leak). Fully closing it requires structural clause parsing across all three phase guards and is out of Phase 8 scope; the Phase 8 helper was kept at the narrow Phase 7 baseline with the limitation documented in its doc-comment.

Verdict ARCHIVE-READY.
